role: Leader of the Worker-communist Party of Iran (WPI); Marxist politician
bio: Hamid Taqvaee is the longtime leader of the [Worker-communist Party of Iran (WPI)](/orgs/worker-communist-party-of-iran), the principal organisation of the Iranian worker-communist movement founded by [Mansoor Hekmat](/people/mansoor-hekmat). After Hekmat's death in 2002, Taqvaee emerged as the dominant figure in the WPI, steering it through the 2004 factional split that produced the rival [Hekmatist Party](/orgs/hekmatist-party) led by [Koorosh Modarresi](/people/koorosh-modarresi). Under Taqvaee's leadership the WPI has maintained a strong secularist and internationalist stance, opposing both the Islamic Republic and imperialist intervention. He is one of the most prominent voices of the Iranian Marxist left in exile.
editorial_notes: WPI leader after Mansoor Hekmat's death 2002. Led WPI through 2004 Hekmatist split. Based in exile.
